meta.description_1l: Linux keyboard layout switcher that auto-corrects EN/RU input, typos, and case mistakes.
meta.description_short: Punto is a Linux desktop utility built around a root-owned keyboard daemon and an optional tray app. The daemon reads interception-tools input streams, analyzes completed words with dictionary and n-gram heuristics, and retypes corrected text through X11 and uinput. The tray app edits user settings, toggles auto-switching over a Unix socket, and syncs the system layout hotkey for GNOME or generic X11.

system.name: Punto Switcher for Linux
system.description: Local-only Linux utility that intercepts keyboard input, fixes wrong EN/RU layout input, case glitches, and typos, and exposes tray-based control plus install-time tooling.

users.name: Desktop User
users.description: Types text, uses Pause-based corrections, and toggles behavior from the tray icon.

users.name: Local Administrator
users.description: Builds or installs the package, manages udevmon/systemd integration, and maintains default config.

system.top_boundary: Runs locally as a root-owned keyboard daemon, an optional GTK tray controller, and Debian-oriented packaging/runtime scripts on X11 Linux desktops.

containers.name: Keyboard Daemon
containers.description: Consumes intercepted keyboard events, analyzes completed words, and injects corrected output back into the desktop session.
responsibilities: Read keyboard events from stdin in an interception-tools pipeline
Track word boundaries, modifier state, and rollback history
Run async language, typo, and sticky-shift analysis
Rewrite text through clipboard, paste, backspace, and layout macros
Expose local IPC for enable, disable, status, and reload operations

containers.name: Tray UI
containers.description: GTK/AppIndicator frontend that surfaces daemon status, user settings, and system hotkey synchronization.
responsibilities: Show service state in the system tray
Toggle auto-switching and sound without restarting udevmon
Edit per-user YAML settings
Validate and apply layout hotkeys to GNOME or generic X11 backends

containers.name: Distribution Tooling
containers.description: Shell scripts, package metadata, and static assets that build, install, and operate the daemon and tray binaries.
responsibilities: Build native binaries and assemble a Debian package
Install default config, tray autostart, and udevmon assets
Start, stop, and restart backend and tray processes from a CLI wrapper
Refresh runtime dependencies during package creation

components.name: Daemon Bootstrap
components.description: Loads config, installs signal handlers, and creates the main EventLoop instance.
responsibilities: Prefer user config over the system config when loading defaults
Validate core hotkey and auto-switch ranges
Wire process lifecycle flags and startup logging
Pass the current Config snapshot into EventLoop

data_flows.payload: Config values for hotkeys, auto-switching, typo correction, and sound

components.name: Event Orchestrator
components.description: Central runtime loop that tracks typed state, coordinates corrections, and executes output macros safely across multiple daemon instances.
responsibilities: Read and classify input_event frames from stdin
Maintain current word, trailing whitespace, and rollback history
Coordinate undo detection and cross-process macro locking
Apply manual and automatic correction actions in order

data_flows.payload: linux input_event frames for one physical keyboard
WordResult with target layout and optional corrected KeyEntry sequence
clipboard writes, paste macros, layout switch requests, and sound cues

components.name: Analysis Pipeline
components.description: Dictionary-first asynchronous word analysis that falls back to n-grams and typo heuristics when a decision is ambiguous.
responsibilities: Load English and Russian dictionaries with Bloom-filter prechecks
Score ambiguous words with bigram and trigram tables
Detect sticky-shift patterns and typo candidates
Run word analysis on background worker threads

data_flows.trigger: word committed at space or tab boundary
data_flows.payload: WordTask with KeyEntry buffer, current layout, and AutoSwitchConfig snapshot
WordResult with correction type, optional replacement keys, and timing telemetry

components.name: Text Transforms
components.description: Pure UTF-8 conversion helpers for layout inversion, transliteration, case inversion, and visible-text rendering.
responsibilities: Map QWERTY scancodes between English and Russian layouts
Invert per-character case for Latin and Cyrillic text
Transliterate text between Cyrillic and Latin alphabets
Render KeyEntry sequences into visible text for oneshot paste paths

components.name: Desktop Integration
components.description: Bridges the root-run daemon into the active desktop session for X11, clipboard ownership, terminal-safe paste, and sound playback.
responsibilities: Discover the active GUI session and export DISPLAY/XAUTHORITY
Read and set the current keyboard layout
Own and restore X11 clipboard and primary selections
Detect terminal windows to choose a safe paste shortcut
Play layout-switch sounds in the active user session

data_flows.from: X server and active desktop session
data_flows.payload: DISPLAY, XAUTHORITY, active window metadata, and layout state

components.name: Daemon IPC
components.description: Background Unix-socket server that publishes enablement state and accepts control commands from local clients.
responsibilities: Bind the primary control socket or a per-process fallback socket
Accept GET_STATUS, SET_STATUS, RELOAD, and SHUTDOWN commands
Forward reload requests to a thread-safe callback
Expose daemon state to tray and local tooling

data_flows.payload: service status queries, enable/disable toggles, and config reload requests

components.name: Tray Shell
components.description: GTK/AppIndicator application shell that renders menu state and dispatches user actions.
responsibilities: Bootstrap GTK and AppIndicator
Poll daemon status on a timer and update tray icons
Offer auto-switch, sound, settings, about, and quit actions
Coordinate settings saves with daemon reloads

data_flows.payload: toggle requests, settings dialog opens, and quit actions

components.name: Tray IPC Client
components.description: Local client that discovers daemon sockets, broadcasts commands, and aggregates multi-instance daemon state.
responsibilities: Scan /var/run for primary and fallback daemon sockets
Send GET_STATUS, SET_STATUS, and RELOAD commands with timeouts
Merge responses from multiple daemon processes into one tray status

data_flows.payload: Unix socket request strings and aggregated daemon replies

components.name: Tray Settings
components.description: Loads and saves per-user settings and syncs the system layout hotkey through GNOME or X11 backends.
responsibilities: Parse and write ~/.config/punto/config.yaml
Render settings dialog fields for auto-switch, typo fix, sound, and hotkeys
Validate requested system layout shortcuts before saving
Write layout-toggle settings through GSettings or setxkbmap

data_flows.trigger: tray startup or settings dialog open
data_flows.payload: user overrides for auto-switch, typo correction, sound, and hotkeys

components.name: Packaging and Runtime Scripts
components.description: Bash and Debian metadata that compile the project, assemble installation assets, and control deployed processes.
responsibilities: Install build and runtime packages before packaging
Compile punto and punto-tray with CMake
Assemble Debian metadata, static assets, and default config
Install or refresh udevmon and tray autostart files
Expose start, stop, restart, and status commands for deployed binaries

data_flows.payload: package build flags, systemd control requests, and asset installation

external.name: interception-tools
external.purpose: capture physical keyboard events and replay corrected events through uinput

external.name: X11
external.purpose: active session discovery, clipboard ownership, and keyboard layout control

external.name: XCB
external.purpose: optional X11 integration enabled when available

external.name: Hunspell
external.purpose: dictionary-backed word validation and suggestion generation

external.name: GTK3
external.purpose: tray menus, dialogs, and desktop UI widgets

external.name: GLib / GSettings
external.purpose: GNOME hotkey inspection and update logic

external.name: systemd
external.purpose: udevmon lifecycle control and loginctl session inspection

external.name: pulseaudio-utils / alsa-utils
external.purpose: optional sound playback for layout-switch cues

data_structures.name: AutoSwitchConfig
data_structures.description: Runtime thresholds and feature flags that govern automatic word analysis and correction behavior.

fields.enabled: bool - master switch for auto layout correction
fields.threshold: double - minimum score ratio before switching layouts
fields.min_word_len: size_t - shortest word allowed into analysis
fields.min_score: double - floor for confident n-gram decisions
fields.max_rollback_words: size_t - maximum number of recent words that replay logic may rewrite
fields.typo_correction_enabled: bool - enables typo suggestions and replacements
fields.max_typo_diff: size_t - edit-distance ceiling for typo fixes
fields.sticky_shift_correction_enabled: bool - enables title-case repair for sticky-shift patterns

data_structures.name: Config
data_structures.description: Top-level daemon configuration loaded from the system or user YAML file.

fields.hotkey: HotkeyConfig - modifier plus key used as a layout-toggle fallback
fields.auto_switch: AutoSwitchConfig - automatic analysis and correction rules
fields.config_path: filesystem::path - resolved file path used for this config snapshot

data_structures.name: KeyEntry
data_structures.description: Normalized keyboard token that preserves scan code and whether Shift was held when the key was typed.

fields.shifted: bool - whether the visible character was shifted

data_structures.name: AnalysisResult
data_structures.description: Scoring outcome from dictionary and n-gram language analysis for a completed word.

fields.should_switch: bool - whether the daemon should switch layouts

data_structures.name: WordTask
data_structures.description: Queued work item representing one completed word handed from the EventLoop to the async analysis pool.

fields.word: vector<KeyEntry> - full typed token buffer including punctuation
fields.analysis_len: size_t - prefix length that should be analyzed
fields.layout_at_boundary: int - keyboard layout seen at the word boundary
fields.cfg: AutoSwitchConfig - analysis rules snapshot for this task

data_structures.name: WordResult
data_structures.description: Async analysis result returned to the EventLoop with optional replacement keys and telemetry.

fields.need_switch: bool - whether the resulting correction needs a layout change
fields.correction_type: CorrectionType - no-op, layout switch, typo fix, sticky-shift fix, or combined fix
fields.correction: optional<vector<KeyEntry>> - replacement word when the output differs from the original
fields.layout_at_boundary: int - layout snapshot from the task
fields.queue_us: uint64_t - microseconds spent waiting in the queue

data_structures.name: HistoryWord
data_structures.description: Metadata for one committed word inside the rollback/replay history ring buffer.

fields.task_id: uint64_t - analysis task linked to the committed word
fields.end_pos: uint64_t - exclusive token-stream end position before the delimiter
fields.delim_pos: uint64_t - token position of the committed delimiter
fields.delimiter: ScanCode - recorded space or tab token
fields.detected_language: uint8_t - contextual language tag for recent-word heuristics

data_structures.name: X11SessionInfo
data_structures.description: Snapshot of the active desktop session that the root-run daemon must impersonate for X11 work.

fields.session_id: string - systemd login session identifier when available
fields.uid: uint32_t - target effective UID for X11 calls
fields.gid: uint32_t - target effective GID for X11 calls
fields.display: string - X11 display name such as :0
fields.xauthority_path: string - Xauthority file used to reach the X server
fields.xdg_runtime_dir: string - runtime directory for the active session

data_structures.name: SettingsData
data_structures.description: User-facing settings model stored by the tray dialog before it is written back to YAML or applied to the system hotkey backend.

fields.auto_enabled: bool - tray-side toggle for automatic layout switching
fields.threshold: double - analysis threshold mirrored from config.yaml
fields.min_score: double - minimum score for a confident correction
fields.max_rollback_words: int - rollback depth for replay-based correction
fields.max_typo_diff: int - maximum edit distance for typo repair

external_services.name: udevmon
external_services.purpose: Hosts the interception pipeline and launches one daemon process per keyboard device.
external_services.auth: Local root or systemd service permissions
external_services.trigger: Package install, CLI start, or system boot

external_services.name: X Server
external_services.purpose: Clipboard access, active-window inspection, and XKB layout control.
external_services.auth: DISPLAY and XAUTHORITY from the active desktop session
external_services.trigger: Clipboard-based paste, layout switching, terminal detection, and tray UI startup

external_services.name: systemd / loginctl
external_services.purpose: Discover the active desktop session and manage the udevmon service lifecycle.
external_services.trigger: Daemon session refresh and CLI/package lifecycle actions

external_services.name: GNOME GSettings
external_services.purpose: Read and write the system layout-switch hotkey on GNOME desktops.
external_services.trigger: Saving tray settings when the active backend is GNOME

external_services.name: setxkbmap
external_services.purpose: Fallback writer for X11 layout-toggle configuration outside GNOME.
external_services.trigger: Saving tray settings when the active backend is generic X11

external_services.name: paplay or aplay
external_services.purpose: Play optional sound cues after a successful layout change.
external_services.trigger: Layout switch when sound is enabled

glossary.definition: Automatic correction that runs after a word boundary such as Space or Tab.
One typed token stored as a scan code plus its Shift state.
Correction strategy that deletes already typed tokens and retypes the corrected sequence.
Wrong-case pattern like ПРивет or кОЛБАСА that should become Title Case.
Hunspell-assisted correction for insertions, deletions, substitutions, and adjacent transpositions.
Russian words represented as the English keyboard sequence that produced them, used for lookup and conversion.
Local Unix domain socket under /var/run that carries status and reload commands between tray and daemon.
Cross-process flock-based guard that prevents multiple daemon instances from replaying corrections at the same time.
Clipboard-plus-paste replacement path used instead of retyping characters one by one.
Heuristic that skips case-only fixes for words that should not receive sticky-shift corrections.
